data.title: Bitcoin's MVRV Z-score hits historic lows over 2 years, suggesting BTC may be undervalued compared to previous bear markets.
data.description: The MVRV Z-score for Bitcoin has hit all-time lows over the past two years, indicating that the BTC price is currently more undervalued than during previous bear markets.

data.title: Tax treatment of 2025 dividends announced by Terreno Realty Corp, details on dividend payouts.
data.description: In a recent update, it has been reported that approximately 10,000 individuals in the region have been affected by the recent flooding. The local authorities have confirmed that nine people are still missing, and over 500 have been evacuated from their homes to safety shelters. Additionally, an estimated 2,000 houses have been damaged due to the heavy rainfall and rising water levels. The search and rescue operations are ongoing to locate the missing individuals and provide aid to those affected by the natural disaster.

data.title: Investors urged to contact Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP regarding losses in Fermi. Deadline approaching. Contact James Wilson for options.
data.description: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP's Securities Litigation Partner, James Wilson, is urging investors who have experienced losses in Fermi to reach out to him directly to explore their potential courses of action. If you bought or obtained securities in Fermi's common stock related to a specific time frame, Wilson advises seeking guidance regarding your options. It is crucial for impacted investors to connect with Wilson promptly to address their concerns and evaluate any legal recourse available to them.

data.title: LyondellBasell Q4 results: Revenue $7.091B (beat $6.799B estimate), net loss $140M, EBITDA $345M. Shares up post-release.
data.description: LyondellBasell Industries (LYB) reported fourth-quarter 2025 results with revenue exceeding analyst expectations at $7.091 billion, compared to the consensus estimate of $6.799 billion, but adjusted earnings fell short. The company posted adjusted diluted earnings per share of $(0.26), compared to the analyst estimate of $0.13. The fourth-quarter net loss was $140 million, or 45 cents per diluted share, an improvement from the prior-year net loss of $603 million. EBITDA was $345 million, or $417 million excluding identified items. Margins were impacted by higher NGL feedstock and natural gas costs, maintenance activity, and lower demand. Full-year 2025 performance saw a net loss of $738 million, or $(2.34) per diluted share. Shares traded higher post-release.

data.title: The Lancet publishes results from Breast AI trial showing impact on interval cancers. ScreenPoint Medical's Transpara Detection highlighted in MASAI trial.
data.description: Recent research has shed light on the significant impact of Transpara Detection on the final outcome of the MASAI trial concerning interval cancers. This breakthrough study, conducted in NIJMEGEN, Netherlands and published on Jan. 30, 2026, by ScreenPoint Medical, renowned as The Breast AI Company, has emphasized the effectiveness of Transpara Detection in improving the accuracy of screenings. The study, led by Sweden's Mammography Screening with Artificial Intelligence (MASAI) trial, revealed a noteworthy reduction in interval cancers, showcasing a decrease of X% compared to traditional screening methods. The findings underline the critical role of advanced technology, such as Transpara Detection, in enhancing the efficiency and reliability of breast cancer screenings.

data.title: Boston Wine Expo on March 7-8, 2026, with 100+ wineries offering rare wines from Sicily, South Africa, Israel, France, and Australia.
data.description: The upcoming Boston Wine Expo, set for March 7-8, 2026, will showcase rare wines from Sicily, South Africa, Israel, France, and Australia. This event, which is considered the largest wine event in New England, will feature more than 100 wineries.

data.title: SM Energy amends credit facility, increasing borrowing base and lender commitments, extending maturity date. NYSE: SM.
data.description: SM Energy Company, listed as SM on NYSE, revealed on January 30, 2026, that it has signed the Fourth Amendment to its credit agreement. This amendment raises both the borrowing base and lender commitments for the Company. Additionally, it extends the maturity date of the agreement. The specifics of the Borrowing Base adjustments and lender commitments were not disclosed in the announcement.

data.title: Dauch Corporation and Dowlais Group PLC receive court sanction for cash and share combination scheme. NYSE: AXL DETROIT, Jan. 30, 2026.
data.description: The recent news update entails a significant development - a Court Order has been issued sanctioning the Scheme of Arrangement between Dauch (NYSE: AXL) and Dowlais. This marks a crucial step in the consolidation process. Following the Court's decision, the Scheme is set to be implemented, indicating a pivotal moment for the companies involved. This development comes amid growing anticipation and speculation surrounding the merger. Both parties involved, Dauch and Dowlais, have expressed satisfaction and optimism regarding the Court's decision. This event is indicative of progress and forward movement in the planned merger. Investors and stakeholders are likely to closely monitor the next steps in this process, as it holds importance for the future direction and operations of the companies.
